title: Radial bitnet submission
body:
⛳ Experimental 16MB Submission: FRO Stable + Radial + Disciplined Bigram Hash
This PR submits a public experimental entry for the Parameter Golf 16MB track, featuring a compressed dual-branch architecture optimized for sample efficiency under a strict 10-minute training budget.

The reported result of 1.5852 BPB was obtained from a verified development run on Kaggle Dual T4 hardware. This submission is intended as a transparent demonstration of a non-standard architecture (FRO + Radial) tailored for extreme parameter-count constraints.

🛠️ Technical Highlights
This design integrates three original components by the author to maximize information density:

FRO Stable (Fractal Resonant Optimization): The primary optimizer for the transformer core. It modulates update strength via multi-scale directional resonance, ensuring stability in the high-learning-rate, short-horizon regime.
Radial Token Geometry: A lightweight token-level analytic branch. Instead of learned embeddings, it injects geometric features derived directly from token bit-representations through a multi-angle mapping.
Disciplined Bigram-Hash Branch: A 1024-bucket hashed bigram branch that provides fast lexical context. The "Disciplined" variant uses dynamic gain-clamping to prevent branch-collapse during the final warmup-down phase.
Compressed Core: BitNet-style ternary-weight forward behavior in major internal projections and a mixed 

int8
/

int6
 post-training export with strategic pruning.
📋 Configuration
Parameters: 18,476,354 (Forward), compressed via mixed quantization.
Sequence Length / Batch Size: 1024 / 4
Optimization: FRO Stable (core) + AdamW (auxiliary branches).
Hardware: Kaggle Dual T4 (Development Site).
Wallclock: 600.2 seconds.
Audited Artifact Size: 15,943,179 bytes (Passes 16MB limit with 56KB headroom).
📈 Reported Result
Best Observed val_bpb: 1.5852
Final Train Loss: 3.5058
📝 Notes
This submission represents the definitive peak of the author's FRO + Radial + Hash development line. While reproduction on different hardware (e.g., official 8xH100) may show minor variance, the core script includes robust environment-aware torch.autocast (supporting bfloat16) to ensure high-performance execution in the target evaluation environment.

The repository entry includes:


README.md
 (Updated)

submission.json
 (Updated)

train.log
 (Audited)

train_gpt.py
 (DDP Enabled)
